that coordinates task delegation across specialized agents in your enterprise. Itâs defined by the
agents and MCP servers it can use to accomplish tasks. The broker name is used as the `assetId` in
the `exchange.json` file. The `groupId` defaults to the associated business group value used in
`exchange.json`.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>brokers</code></p></td><td><p>The list of brokers defined in your agent network.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with property names matching this pattern: <code>^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*$</code>. The name must start with a letter or an underscore and can contain letters, numbers, underscores, dots, or hyphens. The name cannot be: <code>default</code>, <code>defaults</code>, <code>system</code>, <code>local</code>, <code>null</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

The `broker` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>card</code></p></td><td><p>Agent card definition.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Refer to the <code>card</code> object properties.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec</code></p></td><td><p>Broker specification.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Refer to the <code>spec</code> object properties.</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Card Section

`card`

This section adheres to the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) specification v0.3.0 and describes the brokerâs
contract, skills, and capabilities. This is a standard A2A agent card
[as defined in the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol specification.](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#55-agentcard-object-structure).

The `card` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>protocolVersion</code></p></td><td><p>The version of the A2A protocol this agent supports.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>A2A protocol version (for example, "0.3.0")</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>A human-readable name for the agent.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>A human-readable description of the agent, assisting users and other agents in understanding its purpose.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>url</code></p></td><td><p>The preferred endpoint URL for interacting with the agent. This URL must support the transport specified by 'preferredTransport'.</p></td><td><p>String (URI)</p></td><td><p>Valid URI string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>preferredTransport</code></p></td><td><p>The transport protocol for the preferred endpoint (the main 'url' field).</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>JSONRPC</code>, <code>GRPC</code>, <code>HTTP+JSON</code>. Defaults to <code>JSONRPC</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>additionalInterfaces</code></p></td><td><p>A list of additional supported interfaces (transport and URL combinations).</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of interface objects (See <a href="#additionalinterfaces-properties">AdditionalInterfaces properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>iconUrl</code></p></td><td><p>An optional URL to an icon for the agent.</p></td><td><p>String (URI)</p></td><td><p>Valid URI string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>provider</code></p></td><td><p>Information about the agentâs service provider.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with provider properties (See <a href="#provider-properties">provider properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>version</code></p></td><td><p>The agentâs own version number. The format is defined by the provider.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Version string (for example, "1.0.0")</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>documentationUrl</code></p></td><td><p>An optional URL to the agentâs documentation.</p></td><td><p>String (URI)</p></td><td><p>Valid URI string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>capabilities</code></p></td><td><p>A declaration of optional capabilities supported by the agent.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with capability properties (See <a href="#capabilities-properties">capabilities properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>securitySchemes</code></p></td><td><p>A declaration of the security schemes available to authorize requests.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Keys are scheme names; values follow the OpenAPI 3.0 Security Scheme Object</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>security</code></p></td><td><p>A list of security requirement objects that apply to all agent interactions.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of security requirement objects</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>defaultInputModes</code></p></td><td><p>Default set of supported input MIME types for all skills, which can be overridden on a per-skill basis.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of MIME type strings (for example, <code>["application/json", "text/plain"]</code>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>defaultOutputModes</code></p></td><td><p>Default set of supported output MIME types for all skills, which can be overridden on a per-skill basis.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of MIME type strings (for example, <code>["application/json", "text/plain"]</code>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>skills</code></p></td><td><p>The set of skills, or distinct capabilities, that the agent can perform.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of skill objects (See <a href="#skills-properties">skills properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>supportsAuthenticatedExtendedCard</code></p></td><td><p>Indicates if the agent can provide an extended agent card with additional details to authenticated users.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p><code>true</code> or <code>false</code>. Defaults to <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>signatures</code></p></td><td><p>JSON Web Signatures computed for this AgentCard.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of signature objects</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### `provider` Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>organization</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the organization providing the agent.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>url</code></p></td><td><p>The URL of the organization.</p></td><td><p>String (URI)</p></td><td><p>Valid URI string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### `additionalInterfaces` Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>url</code></p></td><td><p>The URL for this additional interface.</p></td><td><p>String (URI)</p></td><td><p>Valid URI string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>transport</code></p></td><td><p>The transport protocol for this interface.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>JSONRPC</code>, <code>GRPC</code>, <code>HTTP+JSON</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### `skills` Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>id</code></p></td><td><p>Unique identifier for the skill.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>name</code></p></td><td><p>A human-readable name for the skill.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>A description of what this skill does.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>examples</code></p></td><td><p>Usage examples demonstrating how to use this skill.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of example strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>inputModes</code></p></td><td><p>Supported input MIME types for this skill (overrides defaultInputModes).</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of MIME type strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>outputModes</code></p></td><td><p>Supported output MIME types for this skill (overrides defaultOutputModes).</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of MIME type strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tags</code></p></td><td><p>Categorization tags for this skill.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of tag strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### `capabilities` Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>streaming</code></p></td><td><p>Indicates if the agent supports streaming responses.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p><code>true</code> or <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>pushNotifications</code></p></td><td><p>Indicates if the agent supports push notifications.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p><code>true</code> or <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>stateTransitionHistory</code></p></td><td><p>Indicates if the agent maintains state transition history.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p><code>true</code> or <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>extensions</code></p></td><td><p>List of extension capabilities supported by the agent.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of extension objects (See <a href="#extensions-properties">extensions properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### `extensions`

Example

```yaml
brokers:
  employee-onboarding-broker: # The name that the agent (broker) will be referenced by. This value is used as the asset ID in Anypoint Exchange.
    card:
      protocolVersion: 0.3.0 # A2A protocol version that the broker supports.
      name: Employee Onboarding Broker
      description:
        This agent acts as a broker for employee onboarding. It orchestrates the onboarding process
        by leveraging available tools to ensure new employees have access to all of the required
        systems for their daily work.
      url: ${agentregistry.url}/employee-onboarding-broker # URL where the broker is hosted. Represents the preferred endpoint as declared by the broker.
      provider:
        organization: MuleSoft
        url: https://www.mulesoft.com/
      defaultInputModes: # Supported media types for input
        - application/json
        - text/plain
      defaultOutputModes: # Supported media types for output
        - application/json
      skills:
        - id: onboarding-agent
          name: Onboarding Agent
          description:
            This agent acts as a broker. It orchestrates the use of specialized tools and agents,
            including MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, to manage employee onboarding.
          examples: # Usage examples
            - Onboard a new sales employee. Employee name is 'Alex Smith', email is
              'alex.smith@example.com', and phone number is '555-123-4567'.
          inputModes:
            - application/json
            - text/plain
          outputModes:
            - application/json
            - text/plain
          tags: [onboarding] # Categorization tags
      capabilities:
        streaming: false
        pushNotifications: false
        stateTransitionHistory: false
        extensions:
          - uri: "https://example.com/ext/konami-code/v1"
            description: "Description text"
            required: false
            params:
              hints: "Hint text"
      version: 1.0.0 # Agent (broker) version
```

The `extensions` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>uri</code></p></td><td><p>URI identifying the extension.</p></td><td><p>String (URI)</p></td><td><p>Valid URI string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>Description of the extension.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>required</code></p></td><td><p>Indicates if this extension is required.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p><code>true</code> or <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>params</code></p></td><td><p>Parameters for the extension.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Any object (for example, <code>{"hints": "Hint text"}</code>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Spec Section

`spec`

This section configures the brokerâs internal implementation. You can think of this section as the
source code for your broker.

In this section, specify the LLM to use, custom instructions, available tools, and error handling.
The `links` section defines the agents that are available to this broker. Essentially, the `links`
section is where you define the agent network.

The `spec` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>llm</code></p></td><td><p>LLM configuration for the broker.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#llm-section">LLM</a></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>instructions</code></p></td><td><p>Set of custom instructions that will inform the orchestration.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of instruction strings</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links</code></p></td><td><p>Defines the list of agents that this agent connects to.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of link objects (See <a href="#links-section">Links</a>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>maxNumberOfLoops</code></p></td><td><p>The maximum number of steps that a task can take. Useful for keeping orchestrations from running too long and consuming too many tokens.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>25</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>maxConsecutiveErrors</code></p></td><td><p>The maximum number of errors that the orchestrator will attempt to recover from before returning a failed status.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>3</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>taskTimeoutSecs</code></p></td><td><p>A timeout (in seconds) for how long each orchestration task should run.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>60</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tools</code></p></td><td><p>The tools available to this broker.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of tool objects (See <a href="#tools-section">Tools</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>policies</code></p></td><td><p>Policy bindings to be applied.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of PolicyBinding objects (See <a href="#policies-section">Policies</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### LLM Section

`llm`

The value of this section is a reference to one of the LLMs defined in Anypoint Exchange or in the
`llmProviders` section of `agent-network.yaml`. Because itâs a reference, you can choose to share
the same LLM across all the brokers in your agent network. Or, you can have different brokers use
different LLMs to better suit their tasks. If using an LLM Proxy, configure the LLM Proxy as either
an OpenAI or Gemini LLM depending on the proxyâs format.

For more information about supported LLMs, see
[Large Language Models](af-agent-networks#llm-support).

The `llm` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>ref</code></p></td><td><p>The reference to the LLM to be used by the orchestrator.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#llmproviders-section">LLM Providers</a></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>connection.ref</code></p></td><td><p>Connection reference.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#connections-section">Connections</a></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.model</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the model to use.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Model name string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.openAI</code></p></td><td><p>OpenAI specific configuration settings.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with OpenAI settings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.openAI.reasoningEffort</code></p></td><td><p>Constrains reasoning effort for reasoning models. Useful for managing performance and reason-token usage.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>NONE</code> (default for GPT-5.1)</p></li><li><p><code>MINIMAL</code></p></li><li><p><code>LOW</code></p></li><li><p><code>MEDIUM</code> (default for versions previous to GPT-5.1)</p></li><li><p><code>HIGH</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.openAI.temperature</code></p></td><td><p>Controls randomness in the output. Requires GPT-5.1 and <code>reasoningEffort</code> set to <code>NONE</code>.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Any number</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.openAI.topP</code></p></td><td><p>Nucleus sampling parameter. Requires GPT-5.1 and <code>reasoningEffort</code> set to <code>NONE</code>.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Any number</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.openAI.topLogprobs</code></p></td><td><p>Number of most likely tokens to return at each position. Requires GPT-5.1 and <code>reasoningEffort</code> set to <code>NONE</code>.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Any integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.openAI.maxOutputTokens</code></p></td><td><p>Maximum number of tokens to generate.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Any integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini</code></p></td><td><p>Gemini-specific configuration settings.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with Gemini settings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini.thinkingBudget</code></p></td><td><p>Sets a token budget for the reasoning phase. (Applies only to Gemini 2.5 series.)</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>0</code> (Disabled)</p></li><li><p><code>-1</code> (Automatic)</p></li><li><p><code>1024</code> to <code>32768</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini.thinkingLevel</code></p></td><td><p>Controls the depth of the reasoning process. (Applies only to Gemini 3 series.)</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>High</code> (Default)</p></li><li><p><code>Low</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini.temperature</code></p></td><td><p>Controls randomness.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>For Gemini 3 and Gemini 2.5, Google recommends keeping this at <code>1.0</code> (default) to avoid breaking the reasoning chain.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini.topP</code></p></td><td><p>Nucleus sampling parameter.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Any number</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini.responseLogprobs</code></p></td><td><p>Whether to return log probabilities.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>true or false</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration.gemini.maxOutputTokens</code></p></td><td><p>Maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the response.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Any integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Instructions Section

`instructions`

Provides instructions that are specific to the broker. Instructions often focus on business-oriented
concerns. For example, hereâs instructions for an employee onboarding broker.

```yaml
instructions:
  - |
    You're an Employee Onboarding Broker. Coordinate onboarding of a new employee across the systems needed for day-to-day work.
    ## The process for onboarding an employee is:
    - Onboard in HR: Create the new employee record. Fetch the address using available tools. Don't ask for the address unless you're not sure how to get it.
    - Onboard in CRM: Create the employee's CRM profile.
    - Send a Slack status update using SlackMcpServer.send_status_update (include contextId from input).
    - Request an employee laptop in Zendesk and initiate the employee badge request.
    - Send another Slack status update using SlackMcpServer.send_status_update (include contextId from input).
    - IT system setup: Provision the ping ID system for the employee.
    ## Final Response
    - Return a plain-text human-readable summary of all steps and actions taken, formatted as a bulleted list. Don't include tool names in the summary.
```

Hereâs another example of instructions for a customer service broker. This broker coordinates
management of customer-reported incidents.

```yaml
instructions:
  - |
    You're an Incident Management Broker. Your primary responsibility is to coordinate the resolution of incidents reported by customers.
    The process for incident management is:
      1. Fetch CRM case details: Retrieve the latest critical case details for the customer.
      2. Fetch entitlement details: Obtain the customer's entitlement information.
      3. Fetch on-call engineer: Identify the current on-call engineer for the incident.
      4. Create Slack war room and invite on-call engineer: Set up a Slack war room channel and invite the on-call engineer.
      5. Summarize actions: Provide a clear, human-readable summary of the steps performed, including information about the created Slack channel and the on-call engineer assigned.
```

You donât have to provide instructions like "split the message into tasks", or "select the best
tool". The broker does that on its own.

The `instructions` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>instructions</code></p></td><td><p>Set of custom instructions that will inform the behavior of the broker.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of instruction strings. Each item in the array is a string containing custom instructions for the broker</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>instructions[]</code></p></td><td><p>Individual instruction string.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value. Typically contains business-oriented instructions, process descriptions, or guidelines for the brokerâs behavior.</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Tools Section

`tools`

Tools provide agents with external capabilities. When a broker needs to access an external service
thatâs not another agent, it reaches out to an MCP server.

By default, the broker has access to all the tools available on the MCP server. However, most modern
LLMs can only handle around 20 to 25 tools per context before starting to hallucinate. Limit the
available tools to the minimum needed. You can apply that filtering through the allowed list.

Example

```yaml
tools:
  - mcp:
      ref:
        name: talent-pool-mcp # Exchange asset ID of MCP server. By default, the groupId for this asset is the same as that in exchange.json.
      allowed: # Allowlist specific tools
        - TalentPoolMcpServer.match_email_to_address
      headersToPropagate: [Authorization, X-Request-Id] # Optional. Header names to forward when invoking this tool.
  - mcp:
      ref:
        name: slack-mcp
      allowed:
        - SlackMcpServer.send_status_update
```

The `tools` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>mcp.ref</code></p></td><td><p>The reference to the MCP server that is available to this broker.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#mcp-servers-section">MCP Servers</a></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mcp.connection.ref</code></p></td><td><p>Connection reference.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#connections-section">Connections</a></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mcp.allowed</code></p></td><td><p>Filters the list of tools advertised by the MCP server to only those in this list. Mutually exclusive with 'denied'.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of tool name strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>mcp.headersToPropagate</code></p></td><td><p>The names of the request headers to propagate when the broker invokes this tool.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of header name strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Links Section

`links`

This section is the most important part of the spec, as it defines the agents that are available to
a broker. It enables interagent communication and orchestration. The broker relies on the agents
linked here to execute the appropriate actions to complete a userâs goal.

Example 1

```yaml
links:
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: hr-agent # Exchange asset ID of the linked agent. By default, the groupId for this asset is the same as that in exchange.json.
        namespace: # Optional. If the asset was defined in a different business group, set the corresponding groupId here.
      headersToPropagate: [Authorization, X-Correlation-Id] # Optional. Header names to forward when invoking this agent.
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: badging-agent
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: crm-agent
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: zendesk-agent
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: it-agent
```

Keep this section short. Donât add all available agents. Just like `tools`, excess values here lead
to drops in agent network accuracy and determinism.

Instead, use a multi-level hierarchical approach. This favors traceability and management and helps
keep context sizes in check.

Example 2

In this example, the Onboarding Broker orchestrates the process of onboarding a new hire. It
orchestrates work with the IT, HR and security brokers, which also happen to be brokers in the same
agent network.

Assets, IT Stock, HR, Payroll, and Badges agents are also called **leaf agents**. These arenât
brokers but preexisting agents whose purpose isnât to coordinate work, but to act.

![Diagram showing an employee onboarding broker and its agents](_images/af-onboarding-broker-example.png)

The `links` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>links</code></p></td><td><p>Defines the list of agents that this agent connects to. This defines the agent network and enables inter-agent communication and orchestration.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of link objects</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links[]</code></p></td><td><p>A link object in the links array.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with <code>agent</code> property</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links[].agent</code></p></td><td><p>Agent connection configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with agent properties</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links[].agent.ref.name</code></p></td><td><p>Exchange asset ID of the linked agent. This references the <code>assetId</code> that the agent has in Anypoint Exchange, not the human-friendly name.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Asset ID string (for example, "hr-agent", "crm-agent"). By default, the groupId for this asset is the same as that in exchange.json</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links[].agent.ref.namespace</code></p></td><td><p>Business group ID (groupId) where the asset is defined. Use this when the asset was defined in a different business group than the current one.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Group ID string. To find the groupId, look in the <code>dependencies</code> section in <code>exchange.json</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links[].agent.connection.ref</code></p></td><td><p>Connection reference.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#connections-section">Connections</a>. This references a connection defined in the <code>connections</code> section</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>links[].agent.headersToPropagate</code></p></td><td><p>The names of the request headers to propagate when the broker invokes this agent.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of header name strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Policies Section

`policies`

The `policies` section contains the list of policies to apply to the broker. It contains a reference
to the policy that needs to be added as a dependency to the agent network project.

Example

```yaml
policies:
  - ref:
      name: # Exchange asset ID of the policy. By default, the groupId for this asset is the same as that in exchange.json.
      namespace: # Optional. If the policy was defined in a different business group, set the corresponding groupId here.
    configuration:
      policyConfig1: policyConfig1Value
```

Apply policies at the broker or at the connection. Reference the policy definition and apply the
configuration.

For more information about applying policies via Omni Gateway in Local Mode, see:

- [Securing Agent Interactions with Omni Gateway](../gateway/latest/flex-agent-secure)
- [Applying Policies in Local Mode](../gateway/latest/flex-gateway-secure-local)

> [!NOTE] When you add governance policies to your project, those policies persist between
> deployments. However, if you add governance policies at runtime, those policies wonât persist.

The `policies` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>ref</code></p></td><td><p>Policy reference.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#policies-section">Policies</a></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>configuration</code></p></td><td><p>Policy configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Any object</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Handling

Use these properties to handle error scenarios. Define these properties within the `spec` section of
a broker configuration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th><th>Default</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>maxNumberOfLoops</code></p></td><td><p>The maximum number of steps that a task can take. Useful for keeping orchestrations from running too long and consuming too many tokens.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>25</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>maxConsecutiveErrors</code></p></td><td><p>The maximum number of errors that the orchestrator will attempt to recover from before returning a failed status.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>3</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>taskTimeoutSecs</code></p></td><td><p>A timeout (in seconds) for how long each orchestration task should run.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer (seconds)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td><td><p>60</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Error Handling Considerations

- `maxNumberOfLoops`
  - Each step in an orchestration consumes tokens.
  - Complex tasks may require more steps.
  - Simple tasks should complete with fewer steps.
  - The default of 25 maximum loops is suitable for most use cases.

- `maxConsecutiveErrors`
  - Allows the orchestrator to retry failed operations.
  - Prevents infinite retry loops.
  - Default of `3` attempts provides a balance between resilience and failure detection. After
    reaching this limit, the orchestration returns a failed status.

- `taskTimeoutSecs`
  - Applies to each individual orchestration task.
  - Accounts for network latency and external service response times.
  - Default of 60 seconds is suitable for most API-based operations. Tasks exceeding this timeout
    are terminated.

## Agents Section

`agents`

Reference agents defined in a different agent network, or another agent used in your company. Any
agent is valid, as long as it supports A2A as its communication protocol. For more information, see
[af-agent-networks.adoc#a2a-protocol](af-agent-networks#a2a-protocol)

Example

```yaml
agents:
  hr-agent: # Used as the assetId of the asset published to Anypoint Exchange. The default groupId is the associated value used in exchange.json.
    metadata:
      platform: azure
      protocol: a2a
  badging-agent:
    metadata:
      platform: azure
      protocol: a2a
  crm-agent:
    metadata:
      platform: azure
      protocol: a2a
  zendesk-agent:
    metadata:
      platform: azure
      protocol: a2a
  it-agent:
    metadata:
      platform: azure
      protocol: a2a
```

The `agents` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>label</code></p></td><td><p>A label for this agent.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>A description for this agent.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tags</code></p></td><td><p>Tags associated with this agent.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of string values</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>card</code></p></td><td><p>Agent card definition.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#card-section">Card</a></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata</code></p></td><td><p>Agent metadata configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with <code>protocol</code> property</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.platform</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the platform running this agent. For example, Bedrock, AgentForce, etc.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.protocol</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the protocol. For example, a2a.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>a2a</code>, <code>other</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.links</code></p></td><td><p>Defines the list of agents that this agent connects to.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of objects with <code>agent.ref</code> property</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.tools</code></p></td><td><p>Defines the list of tools that this agent connects to.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of objects with <code>mcp.ref</code> property</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## MCP Servers Section

`mcpServers`

This section lists the MCP servers in your agent network. Use these servers to obtain relevant
context and generate notifications. To apply governance to an MCP server using the agent network
file, it must be in your `agent-network.yaml` file or published to Anypoint Exchange as an asset.

Example

```yaml
mcpServers:
  talent-pool-mcp: # Value used as the assetId in exchange.json. The groupId defaults to the associated value used in exchange.json.
    metadata:
      transport: # Communication method
        kind: streamableHttp # HTTP-based transport
        path: /mcp
        # OR
        sse: # Server-Sent Events transport
          ssePath: /sse
          messagesPath: /messages
      tools: # Server-level tool filtering
        allowed: [tool1, tool2]
  slack-mcp:
    metadata:
      transport:
        kind: streamableHttp
        path: /mcp
```

We support both MCP `streamableHttp` and SSE transports, but we recommend `streamableHttp`.

Just like in the `brokers` section, an allowed attribute is available to filter the available tools.
The allowed attribute in the `brokers` section is more granular and applied only to that specific
broker. In the `mcpServers` section, this attribute applies to the MCP server as a whole, filtering
at the group level.

The `mcpServers` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>label</code></p></td><td><p>A label for this MCP server.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>A description for this MCP server.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tags</code></p></td><td><p>Tags associated with this MCP server.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of string values</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata</code></p></td><td><p>MCP server metadata configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#mcp-servers-section">MCP Servers</a></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.transport</code></p></td><td><p>Communication method configuration for the MCP server.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with transport properties</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.transport.kind</code></p></td><td><p>The transport protocol type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>streamableHttp</code>, <code>sse</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.transport.path</code></p></td><td><p>The path for the <code>streamableHttp</code> transport.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value (for example, <code>/mcp</code>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.transport.sse.ssePath</code></p></td><td><p>The SSE path for Server-Sent Events transport.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value (for example, <code>/sse</code>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.transport.sse.messagesPath</code></p></td><td><p>The messages path for Server-Sent Events transport.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value (for example, <code>/messages</code>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.tools</code></p></td><td><p>Server-level tool filtering configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with <code>allowed</code> array property</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata.tools.allowed</code></p></td><td><p>Filters the list of tools advertised by the MCP server to only those in this list.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of tool name strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## LLMProviders Section

`llmProviders`

Configure the LLMs the brokers use for reasoning. For more information about supported LLMs, see
[Large Language Models](af-agent-networks#llm-support).

The `llmProviders` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>llmProviders</code></p></td><td><p>The list of LLM providers defined as part of this network.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with property names matching pattern: <code>^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*$</code> (must start with letter or underscore, can contain letters, numbers, underscores, dots, hyphens). Each property value is an llmEntity object</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>label</code></p></td><td><p>A label for this LLM.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>description</code></p></td><td><p>The description of this LLM.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>metadata</code></p></td><td><p>LLM metadata configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Either WellKnownLLM or CustomLLM object (See <a href="#metadata-properties">metadata properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### `metadata` Properties

The `metadata` property uses a `oneOf` structure, so it must be either a `WellKnownLLM` or a
`CustomLLM` object but not both.

The `WellKnownLLM` object has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>platform</code></p></td><td><p>The LLM platform provider.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>OpenAI</code>, <code>AzureOpenai</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>models</code></p></td><td><p>List of available models for this platform.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of model name strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

The `CustomLLM` object has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>platform</code></p></td><td><p>The LLM platform provider name.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>models</code></p></td><td><p>List of available models for this platform.</p></td><td><p>Array of strings</p></td><td><p>Array of model name strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>transcoder.ref</code></p></td><td><p>Reference to the transcoder policy.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>See <a href="#policies-section">Policies</a></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connections Section

`connections`

The `connections` section defines one or more connections to instances of an agent network asset.
For example, if an agent is deployed multiple times across regions and environments, you can define
a connection for each deployed agent instance, regardless of whether itâs used by a broker or not.

Connections are elements that are deployed, but they donât have Exchange assets that represent them.
Theyâre connected to assets (agents, MCP servers, LLMs). Theyâre deployed in API Manager in the
egress gateway.

To define multiple connections for the same asset, put them in a resource file and reference the
file in this section.

Example

```yaml
connections:
  hr-agent:
    kind: agent
    ref:
      name: hr-agent # Use the same name as in the associated entry in the agents section or use the Exchange asset ID.
    spec:
      url: https://hr-a2a-agent-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/hr-agent/
      authentication:
        kind: oauth2-client-credentials
        token:
          bodyEncoding: form
          timeout: 300
          url: test.com # Use a variable
        clientId: clientId # Use a variable like ${hrAgent.clientId} and define in in exchange.json so the user is prompted for the value at deployment.
        clientSecret: clientSecret # Use a variable

  badging-agent:
    kind: agent
    ref:
      name: badging-agent
    spec:
      url: https://badge-agent-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/badge-agent/
      authentication:
        kind: oauth2-client-credentials
        token:
          bodyEncoding: form
          timeout: 300
          url: test.com
        clientId: clientId
        clientSecret: clientSecret

  crm-agent:
    kind: agent
    ref:
      name: crm-agent
    spec:
      url: https://crm-a2a-agent-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/crm-agent/
      authentication:
        kind: oauth2-client-credentials
        token:
          bodyEncoding: form
          timeout: 300
          url: test.com
        clientId: clientId
        clientSecret: clientSecret

  zendesk-agent:
    kind: agent
    ref:
      name: zendesk-agent
    spec:
      url: https://zendesk-a2a-agent-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/zendesk/
      authentication:
        kind: oauth2-client-credentials
        token:
          bodyEncoding: form
          timeout: 300
          url: test.com
        clientId: clientId
        clientSecret: clientSecret

  it-agent:
    kind: agent
    ref:
      name: it-agent
    spec:
      url: https://ping-id-a2a-agent-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/pingid/
      authentication:
        kind: oauth2-client-credentials
        token:
          bodyEncoding: form
          timeout: 300
          url: test.com
        clientId: clientId
        clientSecret: clientSecret

  my-openAI:
    kind: llm
    ref:
      name: my-openAI
    spec:
      url: https://api.openai.com/v1/
      configuration:
        apiKey: ${openai.apiKey} # Define the API key of an LLM Proxy as <<clientId>>:<<clientSecret>>

  talent-pool-mcp:
    kind: mcp
    ref:
      name: talent-pool-mcp
    spec:
      url: https://talent-pool-mcp-server-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/
      authentication:
        kind: apiKey
        apiKey: ${talentPool.apiKey} # Define variable in exchange.json
        headerName: X-MCP-API-Key # Optional, defaults to Authorization

  slack-mcp:
    kind: mcp
    ref:
      name: slack-mcp
    spec:
      url: https://slack-mcp-server-inkbh1.paga8m.usa-w1.cloudhub.io/
      authentication:
        kind: apiKey
        apiKey: ${slack.apiKey} # Define variable in exchange.json
        headerName: X-API-Key
```

The `connections` element has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>connections</code></p></td><td><p>The list of connections defined as part of this network.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with property names matching pattern: <code>^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*$</code> (must start with letter or underscore, can contain letters, numbers, underscores, dots, hyphens). Each property value is a ConnectionElement object</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>The type of connection.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>agent</code>, <code>llm</code>, <code>mcp</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ref</code></p></td><td><p>Reference to the asset this connection is for.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with reference properties (See <a href="#ref-element">ref properties</a>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ref.name</code></p></td><td><p>Exchange asset ID of the connected asset.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Asset ID string. For agents, use the same name as in the associated entry in the agents section or use the Exchange asset ID. For LLMs, use the name from llmProviders. For MCP servers, use the name from mcpServers</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ref.namespace</code></p></td><td><p>Business group ID (groupId) where the asset is defined. Use this when the asset was defined in a different business group than the current one.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Group ID string. To find the groupId, look in the <code>dependencies</code> section in <code>exchange.json</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec</code></p></td><td><p>Connection specification. Properties vary by connection kind.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with spec properties (See <a href="#spec-section">Spec</a>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.url</code></p></td><td><p>The URL for the connection endpoint.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URL string</p></td><td><p>Yes (for agent and llm), No (for mcp)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication configuration for the connection.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Authentication object (See <a href="#authentication-types">Authentication types</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.kind</code></p></td><td><p>The type of authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>basic</code>, <code>oauth2-client-credentials</code>, <code>apikey-client-credentials</code>, <code>apiKey</code>, <code>in-task-authorization-code</code>, and <code>oauth2-obo</code></p></td><td><p>Yes (when authentication is specified)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.username</code></p></td><td><p>The username for basic authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>Yes (for basic auth)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.password</code></p></td><td><p>The password for basic authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>Yes (for basic auth)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.headerName</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the header in which to set the key. If not specified, 'Authorization' is set by default.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.clientId</code></p></td><td><p>The client ID for OAuth 2.0 or API key client credentials authentication.</p></td><td><p>String or Object</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>API key client requires an object with <code>value</code> and optional <code>name</code> properties.</p></li><li><p>OAuth 2.0 requires a string value.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes (for oauth2-client-credentials and apikey-client-credentials)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.clientSecret</code></p></td><td><p>The client secret for OAuth 2.0 or API key client credentials authentication.</p></td><td><p>String or Object</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>API key client requires an object with <code>value</code> and optional <code>name</code> properties.</p></li><li><p>OAuth 2.0 requires a string value.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Yes (for oauth2-client-credentials and apikey-client-credentials)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.token</code></p></td><td><p>Configuration on how to fetch the OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with token properties</p></td><td><p>Yes (for oauth2-client-credentials)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.token.url</code></p></td><td><p>The URL of the token provider.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URL string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.token.timeout</code></p></td><td><p>Time in seconds to wait for the service to return the token.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Any number</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.token.bodyEncoding</code></p></td><td><p>The encoding format for the token request body.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>form</code>, <code>json</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.scopes</code></p></td><td><p>An array of scopes to request for OAuth 2.0.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of scope strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.authentication.apiKey</code></p></td><td><p>The value of the API key for API key authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string value</p></td><td><p>Yes (for apiKey)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.configuration</code></p></td><td><p>LLM-specific configuration object.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Any object</p></td><td><p>Yes (for llm connections)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.policies</code></p></td><td><p>Policy bindings to be applied to the connection.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of PolicyBinding objects (See <a href="#policies-section">Policies</a>)</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.policies[].ref</code></p></td><td><p>Policy reference.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with <code>name</code> and optional <code>namespace</code> properties</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.policies[].ref.name</code></p></td><td><p>Exchange asset ID of the policy. By default, the groupId for this asset is the same as that in exchange.json.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Asset ID string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.policies[].ref.namespace</code></p></td><td><p>Optional. If the policy was defined in a different business group, set the corresponding groupId here.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Group ID string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>spec.policies[].configuration</code></p></td><td><p>Policy configuration.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Any object</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection Resolution

Connection resolution happens at deployment time. The connection reference can be explicit or
implicit.

#### Explicit

In this example, the connection to use is **weather-agent-connection**. At deployment, it validates
that a connection with the given name exists in the egress gateway on the specified environment.

Example

```yaml
- agent:
    ref:
      name: weather-agent

    connection:
      ref:
        name: weather-agent-connection
```

#### Implicit

If a connection isnât specified, it defaults to a connection whose name matches the target assetâs
name.

In this example, at deployment a connection to the specified agent asset (weather-agent) is searched
for. If it exists, itâs injected. If not, it fails with an exception.

Example

```yaml
- agent:
    ref:
      name: weather-agent
```

### Authentication Types

Connection authentication is always expressed in accordance with
[Open API authentication methods](https://swagger.io/docs/specification/v3_0/authentication/).

These examples show how to define each supported authentication type in YAML.

Both agents and MCP servers support the same authentication types with custom header capabilities
API Key Authentication and Basic Authentication.

#### Basic Authentication

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: basic
  username: "username"
  password: "password"
```

Using custom header:

```yaml
kind: basic
  username: "username"
  password: "password"
  headerName: X-API-Authorization  # Custom header instead of default "Authorization"
```

The `basic` authentication has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>basic</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>username</code></p></td><td><p>The username for authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>password</code></p></td><td><p>The password for authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>headerName</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the header in which to set the credentials. If not specified, <code>Authorization</code> is used.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: oauth2-client-credentials
  clientId: "client_id"
  clientSecret: "client_secret"
  token:
    url: "https://oauth.provider.com/token"
    bodyEncoding: form
    timeout: 300
  scopes: ["read", "write"] # Optional
```

The `oauth2-client-credentials` authentication has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>oauth2-client-credentials</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>clientId</code></p></td><td><p>The client ID.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>clientSecret</code></p></td><td><p>The client secret.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>token</code></p></td><td><p>Configuration for fetching the token.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with token properties</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>token.url</code></p></td><td><p>The URL of the token provider.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URL</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>token.timeout</code></p></td><td><p>Time in seconds to wait for the service to return the token.</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Any number</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>token.bodyEncoding</code></p></td><td><p>The encoding format for the token request body.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>form</code>, <code>json</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scopes</code></p></td><td><p>An array of scopes to request.</p></td><td><p>Array</p></td><td><p>Array of scope strings</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Anypoint Client Credentials

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: apikey-client-credentials
  clientId: "client_id"
  clientSecret: "client_secret"
```

The `apikey-client-credentials` authentication has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>apikey-client-credentials</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>clientId</code></p></td><td><p>The client ID.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with <code>value</code> and optional <code>name</code> (default header name is <code>client_id</code>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>clientSecret</code></p></td><td><p>The client secret.</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Object with <code>value</code> and optional <code>name</code> (default header name is <code>client_secret</code>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### API Key Authentication

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: api-key
  apiKey: ${agent.apiKey} # Define variable in exchange.json
  headerName: X-API-Key # Optional, defaults to Authorization
```

Using custom header:

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: apiKey
  apiKey: ${agent.apiKey}
  headerName: X-Custom-Auth-Token # Custom header name
```

The `apiKey` authentication has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>apiKey</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>apiKey</code></p></td><td><p>The value of the API key.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>headerName</code></p></td><td><p>The name of the header in which to set the key. If not specified, <code>Authorization</code> is used.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### In-Task Authorization Code

Use `in-task authorization code` when the connection needs secondary credentials obtained during a
task using the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. OAuth2 tokens are extracted from message data and
injected into the `Authorization` header for upstream calls. This supports step-up or in-task
authentication (for example, when a user must re-authenticate for a sensitive action). For more
information about the associated policy, see
[A2A In-Task Authorization Code Policy](../gateway/latest/policies-outbound-a2a-intask-authorization-code).

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: in-task-authorization-code
  secondaryAuthProvider: providerName
  authorizationEndpoint: https://oauth.provider.com/authorize
  tokenEndpoint: https://oauth.provider.com/token
  scopes: Read
  redirectUri: https://oauth.provider.com/callback
  responseType: code
  tokenAudience: https://api.example.com/agents/my-agent
  codeChallengeMethod: S256
  bodyEncoding: form
  challengeResponseStatusCode: 200 #Optional, Status code for challenge response. Default: 200.
  tokenTimeout: 300 #Optional. Timeout in seconds for token requests. Default: 300.
```

The `in-task-authorization-code` authentication has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>in-task-authorization-code</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>authorizationEndpoint</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 authorization endpoint URL. Used to generate the authentication challenge.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URL</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tokenEndpoint</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 token endpoint URL. Used to generate the authentication challenge.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URL</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scopes</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 scopes required for step-up authentication.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Space- or comma-separated scope list (for example, <code>openid profile email</code>)</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>redirectUri</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 redirect URI the client uses in the authorization flow.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URI</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>secondaryAuthProvider</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the IdP (for example, <code>okta</code>, <code>auth0</code>). Informational only, for the authentication card.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>responseType</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 response type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Typically <code>code</code>. Default: <code>code</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>codeChallengeMethod</code></p></td><td><p>PKCE code challenge method.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Typically <code>S256</code>. Default: <code>S256</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tokenAudience</code></p></td><td><p>Intended recipient of the token (for example, <code>agent1</code> or API URL).</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>bodyEncoding</code></p></td><td><p>Encoding for the token request body.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>form</code>, <code>json</code>. Default: <code>form</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tokenTimeout</code></p></td><td><p>Timeout in seconds for token requests.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer. Default: 300</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>challengeResponseStatusCode</code></p></td><td><p>HTTP status code returned for auth-required challenge responses. Typically 200 for JSON-RPC compatibility.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>HTTP status code. Default: 200</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth 2.0 OBO Credential Injection

This authentication type supports OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange and Microsoft Entra ID On-Behalf-Of
protocols. For more information about the associated policy, see
[OAuth 2.0 OBO Credential Injection Policy](../gateway/latest/policies-outbound-oauth-obo).

Using OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange:

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: oauth2-obo
  flow: oauth2-token-exchange
  tokenEndpoint: https://oauth.provider.com/token
  clientId: clientId
  clientSecret: clientSecret
  targetType: audience # Optional. Logical identifier (audience) or physical URI (resource). Default: audience.
  targetValue: https://api.example.com/agents/my-agent
  scope: Read # Optional for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange (RFC 8693). Required for Microsoft Entra OBO and CIBA.
  timeout: 5000 # Optional. Timeout for token exchange requests in milliseconds. Default: 10000.
```

Using Microsoft Entra ID On-Behalf-Of:

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: oauth2-obo
  flow: microsoft-entra-obo
  tokenEndpoint: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token
  clientId: clientId
  clientSecret: clientSecret
  scope: api://downstream-client-id/.default # Required for Microsoft Entra OBO flows.
  timeout: 8000 # Optional. Timeout for token exchange requests in milliseconds. Default: 10000.
```

Using OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange with CIBA (client-initiated backchannel authentication):

```yaml
authentication:
  kind: oauth2-obo
  flow: oauth2-token-exchange
  tokenEndpoint: https://oauth.provider.com/token
  clientId: clientId
  clientSecret: clientSecret
  scope: openid # Required for CIBA flow.
  enableCIBA: true # Optional. Enables OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange with CIBA flow. Available only for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange.
  CIBABackchannelEndpoint: https://oauth.provider.com/bc-authorize # Required when enableCIBA is true. The backchannel authentication endpoint.
  loginHintClaim: email # Optional. JWT claim from incoming subject token to send as login_hint in CIBA request. Default: email.
  bindingMessage: "Please approve this request on your device" # Optional. Human-readable message displayed on authentication device.
  timeout: 10000 # Optional. Timeout for token exchange requests in milliseconds. Default: 10000.
```

The `oauth2-obo` authentication has these properties.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Type</th><th>Valid Values</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>kind</code></p></td><td><p>Authentication type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>oauth2-obo</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>flow</code></p></td><td><p>Token exchange flow type.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>oauth2-token-exchange</code>, <code>microsoft-entra-obo</code></p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>clientId</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 client ID for token exchange.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>clientSecret</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 client secret for token exchange.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>tokenEndpoint</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth2 token endpoint URL for token exchange.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URL</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>targetType</code></p></td><td><p>Parameter type for specifying the target service (audience for logical name, resource for physical URI). Used for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>audience</code>, <code>resource</code>. Default: <code>audience</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>targetValue</code></p></td><td><p>Target audience URI or resource URI for the exchanged token. Required for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Valid URI</p></td><td><p>Required when using <code>oauth2-token-exchange</code> with a target</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>scope</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth scope to request. Required for Microsoft Entra OBO (e.g. <code>api://downstream-client-id/.default</code>). Optional for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Any string</p></td><td><p>Required for <code>microsoft-entra-obo</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>timeout</code></p></td><td><p>Timeout for token exchange requests in milliseconds.</p></td><td><p>Integer</p></td><td><p>Positive integer. Default: 10000</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>enableCIBA</code></p></td><td><p>Enables OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange with CIBA flow. Available only for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange.</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>CIBABackchannelEndpoint`</code></p></td><td><p>The backchannel authentication endpoint (<code>bc-authorize</code>) used to initiate the CIBA flow.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code><a href="https://oauth.provider.com/bc-authorize">https://oauth.provider.com/bc-authorize</a></code></p></td><td><p>Required when <strong>enableCIBA</strong> is <code>true</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>loginHintClaim</code></p></td><td><p>JWT claim from the incoming subject token to send as <code>login_hint</code> in the CIBA request. If no hint is specified, <code>email</code> is used.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>preferred_username</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>bindingMessage</code></p></td><td><p>Human-readable message sent as <code>binding_message</code> in the CIBA request and displayed on the authentication device.</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p><code>Please approve the transaction on your device</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## exchange.json File Element

All agent network projects have an `exchange.json` file. This file contains asset metadata available
in Anypoint Exchange after publishing your agent network assets.

Example

```json
{
  "main": "agent-network.yaml",
  "name": "Employee Onboarding Network",
  "classifier": "agent-network",
  "organizationId": "85de5a54-1f33-4ea4-a1bf-8a65bc409179",
  "descriptorVersion": "1.0.0",
  "tags": [],
  "groupId": "85de5a54-1f33-4ea4-a1bf-8a65bc409179",
  "assetId": "employee-onboarding-network",
  "version": "1.0.5",
  "dependencies": [
    {
      "groupId": "85de5a54-1f33-4ea4-a1bf-8a65bc409179",
      "assetId": "hr-agent",
      "version": "1.0.21",
      "classifier": "agent-metadata",
      "packaging": "zip"
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "variables": {
      "openai": {
        "clientId": {
          "description": "OpenAI LLM Client ID",
          "default": ""
        },
        "clientSecret": {
          "description": "OpenAI LLM Client Secret",
          "default": "",
          "secret": true
        },
        "url": {
          "description": "OpenAI URL",
          "default": "",
          "secret": false
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

These key-value pairs are important for your agent network configuration.

groupId

ID of the Anypoint business group that owns your agent network and all the assets derived from it.

assetId

Unique identifier for the agent network project.

dependencies

Existing assets that this network needs to reference.

variables

Nested in the metadata section, defines all the variables whose values shouldnât be hardcoded in the
agent network YAML file. Enter these values when publishing the agent network. Each variable has a
`description` value, a `default` value, and a `secret` value that indicates whether itâs treated as
sensitive or not.

When the agent network is published, all the assets derived from it share the same `groupId`
(business group) defined in the `exchange.json` file.

### Ref Element

The `ref` element is used in different elements in the agent network YAML, and is important for your
agent network configuration.

The `ref` element represents another asset that exists in Exchange. The type of asset referenced
depends on the context (such as agent, MCP server, LLM provider). The semantic is always that of
referencing an asset that exists in Exchange, or will exist when the agent network is published
(meaning, itâs defined in the file). If referenced, itâs in the `dependencies` section of
`exchange.json`.

This example represents a link to an HR agent. The `name` attribute doesnât point to the agentâs
human friendly or logical name (which can change); instead, it references the `assetId` that
`hr-agent` has in Exchange.

```yaml
links:
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: hr-agent
```

The `hr-agent` asset can either exist already, or can be defined in this same YAML. In either case,
the Exchange `assetId` is the key to this reference.

In some cases, the `ref` element needs to point to an asset thatâs defined in a different business
group. For this, the `ref#namespace` attribute can be used to reference the assetâs `groupId`. To
find the `groupId`, look in the `dependencies` section in the `exchange.json` file.

```yaml
links:
  - agent:
      ref:
        name: hr-agent
        namespace: <the groupId>
```
---

# Publish Agent Network Assets

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Build and publish your agent network project as Anypoint Exchange assets. When you publish, an asset
is created for each broker, agent, and MCP server thatâs in your agent network. The asset ID is the
value of the `name` key in `agent-network.yaml`.

For example, suppose you have an agent name of `badging-agent` in `agent-network.yaml`. After itâs
published, the Exchange `assetId` is `badging-agent`.

## Before You Begin

Make sure you review the [prerequisites](af-get-started#before-you-begin).

If you want to publish your project assets to a different business group, update the relevant
`groupId` in `exchange.json`.

## Publish Your Network Using MuleSoft Vibes

MuleSoft Vibes can help you publish your agent network specification to Anypoint Exchange. For more
information, see [Creating API Specs with MuleSoft Vibes](vibes-api-ai-create-spec).

1.  In the Anypoint Code Builder activity bar, click the agent icon
    ![](_images/af-acb-dev-agent-icon.png).
2.  Tell the agent that you want to publish your agent network. MuleSoft Vibes does the rest.

To get started, try one of these suggested messages.

- Publish my Employee Onboarding project.
- Publish my agent network project version 1.0.0.
- Publish the hello-world-network project to Exchange.
- Publish agent network project at /path/to/project with version 2.1.0.

## Publish Your Network Using Anypoint Code Builder

1.  If youâre not logged in already, log in to your Anypoint Platform account.
2.  In your Anypoint Code Builder project, choose one of the following:
3.  In Explorer, right-click a project file and select **Publish Agent Network Assets**.
4.  In the Command Palette, run this command: **MuleSoft: Publish Agent Network Assets**.
5.  In **Publish Agent Network Project Assets**, enter a version for the project and review the
    project metadata. If you want to publish project assets to a different business group, update
    the `groupId` in `exchange.json`.
6.  Select **Publish**.
7.  Your agents, MCP servers, and LLM provider definitions are published to Exchange. An Exchange
    asset is created for each element in your agent network.

Next, discover your agent network assets in **Agents & Tools** in Exchange to get a comprehensive
view of your integration, including agent and MCP server assets. Exchange automatically tags brokers
for easy identification.

## Publish a Network Using the Anypoint CLI

If you run operations within a CI/CD environment, you can use Anypoint CLIâs plugin to set up,
create, build, publish, and deploy agent networks. For more information, see
[Build Agent Networks in a CI/CD Environment](af-build-agent-networks-in-a-ci-cd-environment).

## See Also

- [Deploy Agent Network Instances](af-deploy-agent-network-targets)
---

# Troubleshoot Agent Networks

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Resolve common agent network errors and connectivity issues by enabling verbose logging, message
logging policies, and distributed tracing. Using logs and distributed traces, you can observe agents
and MCP servers and see how requests flow through brokers, which tools and policies apply, and where
failures or latency originate.

> [!NOTE] Use Agent Visualizer to
> [view metrics, logs, and traces](../agent-visualizer/troubleshoot-agent-network) for agents and
> MCP servers in your network.

## Enable Logging to Debug Agent Brokers

Enable logging and log levels from CloudHub 2.0 or Runtime Fabric:

1.  In Runtime Manager, navigate to the application for the agent broker.
2.  Choose one of these options to configure logging:
    - [CloudHub 2.0](../cloudhub-2/ch2-manage-app-logs#configure-log-levels-and-categories)
    - [Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/deploy-to-runtime-fabric#configure-logging-and-log-levels)

3.  Set the log level to Debug for these classes:
    - `com.mulesoft.modules.agent.broker` (For agent broker)
    - `INSECURE-LOGGING` (For content that can potentially contain sensitive information, such as
      LLM reasoning, and inputs and outputs from agents and assets defined in `tools` and `links`)

> [!IMPORTANT] `INSECURE-LOGGING` is very likely to contain sensitive information, such as
> personally identifiable information (PII). Make sure to disable it after troubleshooting is
> completed.

## Enable Message Logging Policy

Edit `agent-network.yaml` to set message logging for MCP servers and agents.

Message logging uses the
[Omni Gateway Message Logging policy](../gateway/latest/policies-included-message-logging). In the
`connection` section of the agent or MCP server instance you want to log, add the following logging
policy inside `policies`:

```yaml
policies: ## add the following inside the policy setting
  - ref:
      name: message-logging
      namespace: business_group_UUID
    configuration:
      loggingConfiguration:
        - itemName: "Payload"
          itemData:
            message: "#[payload]"
            firstSection: true
            secondSection: true
            level: "INFO"
        - itemName: "Headers"
          itemData:
            message: "#[attributes]"
            firstSection: true
            secondSection: true
            level: "INFO"
```

> [!NOTE] On non-US control planes, the value for the `namespace` parameter can change. In Exchange,
